title: Design the IPC surface
description: Define a minimal, typed set of IPC channels and a preload bridge before wiring features, instead of ad-hoc channels.
body: Steps: 1. List the privileged operations the renderer genuinely needs from main (file save, settings, OS dialogs). 2. Define one IPC channel per operation with a typed request/response contract. 3. In preload, expose a named function per operation via contextBridge that invokes its fixed channel. 4. In main, register one validating handler per channel. 5. Keep the renderer free of any raw ipcRenderer/require access. Rollback point: before exposing a new capability (each one widens the attack surface). Validation: the renderer can perform exactly the intended operations and nothing more, and every channel has a validating handler; a generic pass-through 'invoke(channel,args)' bridge fails this design.
